# Legacy Application Discovery Report

**Generated by**: Browser Agent (Playwright)
**Date**: 2026-03-02
**Legacy Application URL**: http://localhost:50586/
**Application Type**: ASP.NET Web Forms Catalog Manager

---

## Executive Summary

This report documents the automated discovery of the legacy eShop Catalog Manager application. The browser agent successfully captured **3 primary workflows** with **7 screenshots** and detailed form/data analysis.

### Key Findings

- **Application Framework**: ASP.NET Web Forms (classic postback model with ViewState)
- **Primary Entity**: Catalog Items (products)
- **CRUD Operations**: Full Create, Read, Update, Delete functionality
- **Data Grid**: 10 catalog items displayed with pagination
- **Form Fields**: 9 fields per item (Name, Description, Brand, Type, Price, Picture, Stock, Restock, MaxStock)

---

## Discovered Workflows

### 1. Catalog List (catalog-list)

**Purpose**: View and manage the list of catalog items

**Key Features**:
- Data grid displaying all catalog items
- Pagination controls (Previous/Next)
- Per-item actions: Edit, Details, Delete
- Create New button

**Captured Data**:
- 10 catalog items with complete field data
- Table structure with 10 columns
- Action links for all CRUD operations

**Screenshots**:
- `catalog-list/screenshots/000_main_list.png` (980KB)

**Data Export**:
- `catalog-list/workflow.json` - Full workflow metadata
- Original `grid-data.json` - Raw table data

---

### 2. Catalog CRUD Operations (catalog-crud)

**Purpose**: Complete lifecycle management of catalog items

**Operations Captured**:

#### CREATE
- **URL**: `/Catalog/Create`
- **Fields**: 9 input fields
- **Form Controls**:
  - Text inputs: Name, Description, Price, Stock, Restock, Maxstock
  - Dropdowns: Brand (select-one), Type (select-one)
  - Submit button
- **Screenshot**: `001_create_form.png` (725KB)

#### EDIT
- **URL**: `/Catalog/Edit/{id}` (tested with ID=1)
- **Fields**: 10 input fields (includes PictureFileName)
- **Pre-populated**: Values loaded from existing item
- **Screenshot**: `002_edit_form.png` (1.1MB)

#### DETAILS (READ)
- **URL**: `/Catalog/Details/{id}`
- **Display**: Read-only view of all 9 item properties
- **Format**: Label-value pairs (dt/dd tags or field-label/field-value classes)
- **Screenshot**: `003_details_view.png` (960KB)

#### DELETE
- **URL**: `/Catalog/Delete/{id}`
- **Confirmation**: Displays item details with confirmation prompt
- **Action**: Submit button to confirm deletion
- **Screenshot**: `004_delete_confirm.png` (991KB)

**Form Field Analysis**:

| Field Name | Type | Create | Edit | Details | Delete |
|------------|------|--------|------|---------|--------|
| Name | text | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Description | text | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Brand | select | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Type | select | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Price | text | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| PictureFileName | text | No | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Stock | text | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Restock | text | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| MaxStock | text | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |

**Web Forms Control Naming Pattern**:
- Prefix: `ctl00$MainContent$`
- Examples: `ctl00$MainContent$Name`, `ctl00$MainContent$BrandDropDownList`

---

### 3. Static Pages (static-pages)

**Purpose**: Navigation and informational pages

**Discovered Pages**:
1. **Previous Page** (`/Default/index/-1/size/10`)
   - Pagination: Previous button functionality
   - Screenshot: `005_page_0_Previous.png` (980KB)

2. **Next Page** (`/Default/index/1/size/10`)
   - Pagination: Next button functionality
   - Screenshot: `006_page_1_Next.png` (769KB)

**Note**: Limited static content detected. Application is primarily CRUD-focused with dynamic pages.

---

## Technical Observations

### ASP.NET Web Forms Patterns

1. **ViewState**: Present (`__VIEWSTATE` hidden field)
2. **Postback**: Classic ASP.NET postback model (`__doPostBack`)
3. **Control Naming**: Server-side control naming convention with `$` separators
4. **Master Pages**: `ctl00$MainContent$` prefix indicates ContentPlaceHolder usage

### Data Structure

**Catalog Item Schema** (derived from grid and forms):

```typescript
interface CatalogItem {
  id: number;           // Primary key (from URLs /Edit/1, /Delete/1)
  name: string;         // Product name
  description: string;  // Product description
  brand: string;        // Brand name (dropdown: ".NET" | "Other")
  type: string;         // Product type (dropdown: "T-Shirt" | "Mug" | "Sheet")
  price: number;        // Decimal price
  pictureFileName: string; // Image filename (e.g., "1.png")
  stock: number;        // Current stock quantity
  restock: number;      // Restock threshold
  maxStock: number;     // Maximum stock level
}
```

**Sample Data**:
- 10 items captured
- Brands: ".NET", "Other"
- Types: "T-Shirt", "Mug", "Sheet"
- Price range: $8.50 - $19.50
- All items have 100 stock, 0 restock, 0 maxStock

### UI Components

**Grid/Table**:
- 10 data columns + 1 action column
- Row actions: Edit | Details | Delete (pipe-separated links)
- Pagination: Previous/Next navigation
- Class: Likely `.esh-table` or similar (eShop-prefixed)

**Buttons**:
- Primary: "Create New" (`.btn.esh-button.esh-button-primary`)
- Submit: Form submit buttons (various names)

**Links**:
- Action links: `.esh-table-link` class
- URL patterns: `/Catalog/{Action}/{Id?}`

## Migration Implications

### Backend (Python + FastAPI)

**Required Endpoints**:

```python
# Catalog CRUD
GET    /api/catalog              # List all items (with pagination)
POST   /api/catalog              # Create new item
GET    /api/catalog/{id}         # Get item details
PUT    /api/catalog/{id}         # Update item
DELETE /api/catalog/{id}         # Delete item

# Reference Data
GET    /api/catalog/brands       # Get brand options
GET    /api/catalog/types        # Get type options
```

**Data Models** (Pydantic):
- `CatalogItemDto` (response)
- `CreateCatalogItemRequest`
- `UpdateCatalogItemRequest`
- `CatalogItemListResponse` (with pagination)

**Database**:
- Table: `CatalogItems` (9 columns)
- Enums/Reference Tables: `Brands`, `Types`

### Frontend (React + TypeScript)

**Pages/Components**:

```
src/
├── pages/
│   └── catalog/
│       ├── CatalogListPage.tsx      # Main grid view
│       ├── CatalogCreatePage.tsx    # Create form
│       ├── CatalogEditPage.tsx      # Edit form
│       ├── CatalogDetailsPage.tsx   # Read-only details
│       └── CatalogDeletePage.tsx    # Delete confirmation
│
└── components/
    └── catalog/
        ├── CatalogGrid.tsx          # Data table component
        ├── CatalogForm.tsx          # Reusable form (Create/Edit)
        ├── CatalogCard.tsx          # Item display card
        └── Pagination.tsx           # Pagination controls
```

**Routes**:
```typescript
/catalog              → CatalogListPage
/catalog/create       → CatalogCreatePage
/catalog/edit/:id     → CatalogEditPage
/catalog/details/:id  → CatalogDetailsPage
/catalog/delete/:id   → CatalogDeletePage
```

**UI Libraries**:
- Table: shadcn/ui `DataTable` or `react-table`
- Forms: `react-hook-form` with Zod validation
- Dropdowns: shadcn/ui `Select` component

---

## Next Steps

### 1. OpenAPI Contract Definition
- Define all 5 CRUD endpoints in `openapi/catalog.yaml`
- Include schemas for CatalogItem, pagination, validation errors

### 2. Backend Implementation
- Create `backend/app/catalog/` module
- Implement `router.py`, `schemas.py`, `service.py`, `models.py`
- Set up SQLAlchemy models and migrations

### 3. Frontend Implementation
- Create React components in `frontend/src/pages/catalog/`
- Generate TypeScript types from OpenAPI spec
- Implement TanStack Query hooks for data fetching

### 4. Parity Testing
- Use captured screenshots as golden baseline
- Create visual regression tests (Playwright)
- Verify data grid output matches captured data
- Test all CRUD workflows end-to-end

### 5. Evidence Documentation
- Create `docs/seams/catalog-crud/evidence/evidence.md`
- Document parity test results
- Include before/after screenshots
- Note any approved differences

---

**UI Elements Discovered**: 39 (on home page)

**Tools Used**:
- Playwright (Python) 1.x
- Chromium browser (headless mode)
- Viewport: 1920x1080

**Configuration**:
- Max depth: 3 levels
- Network idle wait: Yes
- Full page screenshots: Yes
- Grid data capture: Yes

## Glossary

- **CRUD**: Create, Read, Update, Delete
- **ViewState**: ASP.NET Web Forms state management mechanism
- **Postback**: Server-side form submission in Web Forms
- **Master Page**: ASP.NET layout template (indicated by ContentPlaceHolder)
- **Golden Baseline**: Reference implementation for parity testing
